rules.statement: Values declared in operational-register fields of .intent/ artifacts MUST match the Operational-register grammar `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`. Operational-register fields include: `authority`, `phase`, `strength`, `enforcement`, `status`, `class`, and `ordering`. A value such as `authority: Policy` (Title Case) or `phase: AUDIT` (UPPER_CASE) imports the wrong register into an Operational-register field and is governance failure.
rules.rationale: Operational-register values are routing keys joined and dispatched on across Python, YAML, JSON, and SQL. A casing mismatch silently breaks loaders that compare case-sensitively, or propagates inconsistent forms through tolerant loaders. Article I §5 of the Constitution declares the three registers; this rule enforces that operational fields stay in the Operational register. See `.specs/papers/CORE-Vocabulary-Registers.md` for the doctrinal source and the full register-assignment table.

rules.statement: Values declared in diagnostic-register fields of .intent/ artifacts MUST match the Diagnostic-register grammar `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`. Diagnostic-register fields include `severity` and verdict-state fields. A value such as `severity: ERROR` (UPPER_CASE) imports the wrong register into a Diagnostic-register field and is governance failure. (Historical note: the rule ID retains 'uppercase' from its original form; ADR-059 D2 inverted the canonical casing to lowercase — see rationale.)
rules.rationale: ADR-059 D2 replaced the audit_severity enum from UPPER_CASE (INFO, WARNING, ERROR) to lowercase (info, low, medium, high, block). The lowercase form is now canonical across the Python codebase and all .intent/ artifacts. This rule is updated (option 2, issue #453) to enforce the ADR-059 D2 canonical form: diagnostic fields must match `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`. A UPPER_CASE value such as `severity: ERROR` is now the governance failure. Article I §5 of the Constitution declares the three registers; this rule enforces that diagnostic fields stay in the Diagnostic register at its canonical casing. See `.specs/papers/CORE-Vocabulary-Registers.md` for the doctrinal source and the full register-assignment table.
